dag-writer-grassmannian

blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex

## Strategy context
This chapter blueprints the **Grassmannian big-cells** construction (GR-cells / GR-glue): for each
size-`d` index set `I ⊆ Fin r`, an affine chart `U^I ≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_S`, and the **Plücker cocycle**
gluing of these charts via the transition maps `Θ_{I,J}` built from the universal matrix and its
minors. The Lean file `GrassmannianCells.lean` carries a cluster of helper declarations (namespace
`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian`) implementing the matrix-algebra and localization bookkeeping that
the cocycle condition `lem:gr_cocycle` rests on. They are proved in Lean (`leanok`) but have **no
blueprint entry**, breaking the 1-to-1 correspondence. Add a concise blueprint entry for each.

## Required content
Add one concise block (`\definition` / `\lemma` / `\theorem`) for **each** of the following Lean
declarations. Read `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianCells.lean` for each exact signature and
role; **the namespace is `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian`** so `\lean{}` targets are e.g.
`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.cocycleΘIJ`. These are **project-internal, Archon-original**
matrix/localization helpers — **no external citation block required** (no `% SOURCE QUOTE:`).
Where a helper merely restates a Mathlib matrix identity in the project's setting (e.g.
`map_nonsing_inv` mirroring `Matrix.map` commuting with `nonsing_inv`), still write it as a project
block (it has nonzero hypotheses), not a `\mathlibok` anchor.

Matrix/minor bookkeeping:
- `universalMatrix_submatrix_self` — the `I`-indexed column submatrix of the universal matrix on
  chart `I` is the identity (the normalization defining the chart).
- `universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` — image of the universal matrix under `transitionPreMap`.
- `imageMatrix_submatrix_self`, `imageMatrix_submatrix_I` — the analogous submatrix identities for
  the image matrix.
- `mul_submatrix_col` — multiplying a matrix by a column-submatrix equals the column-submatrix of
  the product (a `Matrix.submatrix`/multiplication compatibility). One-line.
- `map_nonsing_inv` — a ring map `f` commutes with `nonsing_inv` of an invertible matrix:
  `f.mapMatrix M⁻¹ = (f.mapMatrix M)⁻¹`. One-line.
- `map_map_eq_of_comp` — functoriality: applying two ring maps in sequence equals applying their
  composite (matrix entrywise). One-line.
- `imageMatrix_map_eq` — naturality of the image matrix under a ring map.
- `transitionPreMap_minorDet` — the value of `transitionPreMap` on the minor determinant `minorDet K`.

Localization plumbing (the doubly-localised rings inverting two minors):
- `awayInclLeft`, `awayInclRight` (definitions) — the `algebraMap`s from a single
  `Localization.Away x` (resp. `Away y`) into the double localization `Away (x·y)`.
- `awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap`, `awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap` — these inclusions compose with
  the base `algebraMap` to give the double-localization `algebraMap`.
- `isUnit_algebraMap_away_left`, `isUnit_algebraMap_away_right` — `x` (resp. `y`) is a unit in
  `Away (x·y)`. One-line each.
- `isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross` — the relevant cross minor becomes a unit after the
  localization inclusion (the invertibility making the transition map defined).
- `inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` — a matrix-inverse cancellation identity `(AB)⁻¹·A·(B'...) ` used to
  collapse a composite of transition matrices (read the statement). One-line.

The cocycle transition maps and their identity:
- `cocycleΘIJ`, `cocycleΘJK`, `cocycleΘIK` (definitions) — the localized transition ring maps
  `Θ_{I,J}`, `Θ_{J,K}`, `Θ_{I,K}` between the doubly-localised rings, each via
  `IsLocalization.Away.lift` of the matching `transitionPreMap`.
- `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` — the image-matrix identity over the triple overlap that powers
  the cocycle condition (`Θ_{I,K} = Θ_{I,J}∘Θ_{J,K}` reduced to a matrix equation).

For each block: a short statement in project notation, `\label{}` (suggest `lem:gr_*` / `def:gr_*`
from the Lean basename), the exact `\lean{}`, accurate `\uses{}`, and a one-to-two line proof or
`Proved directly in Lean.` note (do NOT add `\leanok`).

**Wire `\uses{}`:** after reading the .lean, add edges from `lem:gr_cocycle` (and the cocycle-Θ
definitions) to the helpers their Lean proofs actually invoke, and edges among the helpers
themselves (e.g. `cocycleΘIJ \uses{def:gr_away_incl_left, lem:gr_isunit_...}`). Mathlib anchors
already present in the chapter (`IsLocalization.Away.lift`, `Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul`, etc.) should
be reused via `\uses{}`, not re-authored. Run `leandag build --json`; confirm zero isolated/broken
for this cluster.

## Out of scope
- Do NOT modify existing meaningful statements (`def:gr_affine_chart`, `def:gr_universal_matrix`,
  `def:gr_transition`, `lem:gr_cocycle`, `def:gr_glued_scheme`, etc.) beyond adding `\uses{}` edges.
- Do NOT add `\leanok`. Do NOT touch other chapters. Do NOT author Mathlib anchors for these
  project helpers (they are project-proved, not Mathlib re-exports).

## References
None required — these blocks are project-internal matrix-algebra/localization helpers with no

63	
64	# Blueprint Writer
65	
66	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
67	
68	## Your Job
69	
70	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
71	
72	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Blueprint Writer Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Target chapter
83	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
84	
85	## Strategy context
86	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
87	
88	## Required content
89	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
90	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
91	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
92	- ...
93	
94	## Out of scope
95	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
96	
97	## References
98	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
99	- ...
100	
101	## Expected outcome
102	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
103	```
104	
105	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
106	
107	## Chapter format
108	
109	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
110	
111	```latex
112	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
113	  \label{thm:some_label}
114	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
115	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
116	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174  (read from references/hartshorne-III-5.md)
117	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A morphism $f: X \to Y$ of schemes locally of finite
118	  % type is said to be smooth at $x \in X$ if there exist an open affine
119	  % neighborhood $V = \Spec B$ of $f(x)$ and an open affine neighborhood
120	  % $U = \Spec A$ of $x$ with $f(U) \subset V$ such that ..."
121	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, III.5.1.}
122	  Informal statement of the theorem, in the project's notation.
123	\end{theorem}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Proof. We may assume $Y = \Spec B$ and
126	% $X = \Spec A$ are affine. Then $f$ corresponds to a ring homomorphism
127	% $\varphi: B \to A$, and $f$ is smooth at $x$ if and only if ..."
128	\begin{proof}
129	  \uses{thm:another_result}
130	  Step-by-step informal proof, in the project's notation. Reference blueprint labels
131	  with \uses{...} so the dependency graph stays accurate.
132	\end{proof}
133	```
134	
135	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
136	
137	### Citation discipline (the hard rule)
138	
139	**Every declaration block that derives from external reference material requires three citation elements:**
140	
141	1. **`% SOURCE:` LaTeX comment** — pointer + local file. Format: `% SOURCE: <citation pointer> (read from references/<file>.md)`. The pointer is the source identifier + section/theorem/definition number + page when available (e.g. `[Hartshorne], III.5.1, p. 174` or `[Stacks Project], Tag 01V4`). The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical names the local file you opened to produce the verbatim quote.
142	2. **`% SOURCE QUOTE:` LaTeX comment** — the **verbatim text** of the cited statement, copied character-by-character from the local reference file you just named in the `% SOURCE:` line. Verbatim means:
143	   - **Original language.** French for Bourbaki / EGA, German for Grothendieck's early notes, English for Hartshorne / Vakil / Stacks, etc. Do NOT translate.
144	   - **Original notation preserved.** If the source writes $\mathcal{O}_X^*$ and the project uses $\mathcal{O}_X^\times$, the verbatim quote keeps $\mathcal{O}_X^*$. The project-notation restatement happens in the prose body that follows, NOT in the quote.
145	   - **Every word, every symbol.** No paraphrase, no "for brevity" abbreviation. The quote is the anti-hallucination signal — a writer who actually opened the source can paste; a writer reconstructing from memory cannot reproduce the source's exact words.
146	3. **Visible `\textit{Source: <pointer>.}` line** — first line of the prose body. Renders in the PDF so a mathematician reading the typeset blueprint sees the citation at a glance.
147	
148	**For proof blocks**: include a `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` LaTeX comment **immediately before** the `\begin{proof}` environment (NOT inside it). Same verbatim rules — original language, original notation, every word. The informal proof body inside `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}` is the project's restated version in project notation, what the prover formalizes.
149	
150	When a source proof is too long for one verbatim block (multi-page construction): split the theorem into sub-lemmas in the directive's logical structure, and give each sub-statement its own `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`. One opaque mega-quote defeats verifiability. If even sub-splitting is impractical, report it in "Notes for Plan Agent" — do not silently drop the verbatim quote.
151	
152	**For Archon-original / project-bespoke results** (the directive does not name an external source for this block): the source lines are omitted — the block stands on the proof sketch alone.
153	
154	**The hard rule, explicit:**
155	
156	> **You may NEVER write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from training memory.** Every such line must be backed by a local file under `references/` that you have **opened and read in this session**, and the verbatim quote must be a character-by-character copy of text from that file.
157	
158	The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical in `% SOURCE:` is your discipline check. If, when reviewing your own draft, you find a block where you cannot point to the specific local file you read to produce the quote — you fabricated it. Delete the quote. Either dispatch a `reference-retriever` (see below) to obtain the missing source, or report the block as INCOMPLETE under "Notes for Plan Agent".
159	
160	If the directive named a source but the local reference file is missing or doesn't contain the specific statement you need:
161	
162	- **Dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** (see "Dispatching a reference-retriever" below) to fetch the missing material into `references/<slug>.md`.
163	- **Wait for the retriever to return**, then **open and read the new file**.
164	- THEN write the citation block, citing the new local file in the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
165	
166	If retrieval fails (paywall, broken link, no API key, not available online): mark the block `% SOURCE: <pointer> (verbatim text not yet retrieved)` and skip it. Report it as INCOMPLETE. Do NOT substitute a paraphrase, a recollection, or a translation as the verbatim quote — that's the iter-149 failure mode and the entire rule exists to prevent it.
167	
168	## Rules
169	
170	### What you CAN do
171	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
172	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
173	- **Add, correct, or complete `\uses{...}` cross-references** so the dependency edges match the real mathematics. If your chapter's proof of `T` invokes lemma `L`, `T` must declare `\uses{lem:L}`; if a `\uses{}` points at a label that no longer exists, fix it. Verify with `leandag` (see "Verifying dependencies with leandag" below) — do not eyeball it.
174	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
175	- **Write entries for Lean helper declarations.** The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: when the directive lists a helper that exists only in Lean (a prover-created lemma, an instance, a decomposition step), give it a real entry — concise informal statement, `\label{}`, `\lean{}` with the exact Lean name, accurate `\uses{}`, and at least a one-line informal proof. "It's trivial" is not a reason to skip it: the entry is what carries the dependency edges and what helps a later prover fill the sorry. When the helper's Lean proof relies on a fact with no blueprint entry, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" (or create it too, if it belongs in your chapter).
176	- **Author a *Mathlib dependency anchor* and mark it `\mathlibok`** when your chapter relies on a result Mathlib already provides — write out the statement and point `\lean{}` at the real Mathlib declaration (see "Mathlib dependency anchors" below). This is the only marker you may add.
177	- **Remove a declaration block ONLY when the directive explicitly lists it for removal** (typically an isolated/orphaned statement the reviewer flagged `remove`). Default to wiring an isolated node into the graph (add the missing `\uses{}`), not deleting it — an isolated node is usually a missing edge, not dead weight. Never cite from memory — only from a local `references/<file>.md` you opened and read in this session.
186	- **Document every change** in your report, including which `references/<file>.md` files you opened (under "References consulted").
187	
188	### What you MUST NOT do
189	- **Do NOT reference project history.** No "since iteration N," "after our failed attempt," or session narrative. The blueprint must read as a standalone mathematical document.
190	- **Do NOT include Lean implementation details.** No tactic suggestions, typeclass wiring notes, or Lean code structure hints in the prose. If a Lean note is needed, it belongs in `.lean` files (the plan agent will use `lean-scaffolder` for that).
191	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` markers.** `\leanok` is earned by a sorry-free Lean proof and set by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase — never by you. (`\mathlibok` is different: you MAY add it, but ONLY on a Mathlib dependency anchor — see "Mathlib dependency anchors" below — never on the project's own to-be-proved declarations.)

194	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
195	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
196	- **Do NOT write literal `REF` placeholders.** Never produce prose like "Definition~REF" or "Sections REF–REF". Cross-reference declarations with `\cref{<label>}` using the real label (look it up in the chapter or via `leandag`); when the target genuinely has no label yet, describe it by name in prose. 200	- **Do NOT touch protected blocks.** Your invocation prompt's "Protected by the mathematician" section lists `archon-protected.yaml` rules. A `statement`-protected label's declaration block (statement + `\label`/`\lean`/`\uses`) is frozen — you may still write its `\begin{proof}`; an `all`-protected label is entirely off-limits. You may freely `\uses{}` protected labels from other blocks. If your directive conflicts with a protection rule, stop and report the conflict instead of editing.
201	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
202	- **Do NOT fabricate citations.** Never write `% SOURCE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE:`, `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:`, or `\textit{Source: ...}` from memory. If you don't have a local `references/<file>.md` containing the source text, dispatch a retriever and wait. The `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical is the discipline check — if you cannot truthfully point to the file you read, the citation block is not allowed in the chapter.
203	- **Do NOT translate or restate the verbatim quote.** `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` contain the source's original language, original notation, every word as-is. Project-notation rewrites belong in the rendered prose body, not in the verbatim comment.
204	
205	## Mathlib dependency anchors (`\mathlibok`)
206	
207	When your chapter's proofs rely on a definition or lemma that **Mathlib already provides** (and the project will not re-prove), it is good practice — not just a bare `\uses{}` to nowhere — to make that dependency a first-class blueprint block: a **Mathlib dependency anchor**. Doing so:
208	
209	- makes the reliance explicit and readable (a mathematician sees exactly which Mathlib result the route stands on);
210	- gives `leandag` a real node, so the `\uses{}` resolves (no broken/unknown ref) and the node's effort is **done** rather than ∞;
211	- keeps it clear how much the route leans on Mathlib vs. on original work.
212	
213	Write the anchor like any statement block, with three differences:
214	
215	```latex
216	\begin{lemma}[Hensel's lemma]
217	  \label{lem:hensel_mathlib}
218	  \lean{Mathlib.RingTheory.Henselian.foo}   % the REAL Mathlib declaration
219	  \mathlibok
220	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
221	  Statement of the result in the project's notation.
222	\end{lemma}
223	```
224	
225	- **`\lean{}` names the actual Mathlib declaration** (with its real namespace), so `leandag` matches the node to Mathlib.
226	- **`\mathlibok`** marks it as supplied by Mathlib — the DAG treats it as done; no `\begin{proof}` is needed.
227	- No `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` block — Mathlib is the source; the `\lean{}` target *is* the citation.
228	
229	**The anti-hallucination rule (mirrors citation discipline):** mark `\mathlibok` ONLY when the statement genuinely exists in Mathlib and your stated form is faithful to it. A wrong or invented `\mathlibok` is worse than an ∞ hole — the loop will treat a non-existent result as available and skip proving a real gap. So:
230	
231	- Prefer anchors the **directive names** (the plan agent/reviewer identified them as Mathlib results).
232	- If you are not confident the exact statement is in Mathlib, do **not** mark `\mathlibok` — write the block without it (or leave the `\uses{}` for the reviewer to resolve) and flag it under "Notes for Plan Agent" so the reviewer can verify against Lean.
233	- Never put `\mathlibok` on one of the project's **own** to-be-proved declarations. That marker is for Mathlib-supplied results only; `\leanok` (project proofs) is never yours to add.
234	
235	## Reading references
236	
237	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
238	
239	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
240	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
241	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
242	
243	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
244	
245	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
246	
247	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
248	
249	Conditions for dispatch:
250	
251	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
252	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
253	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
254	
255	Dispatch (Bash; treat as blocking and await the report; in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
256	
257	```
258	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
259	  --name reference-retriever \
260	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
261	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
262	  --write-domain 'references/**'
263	```
264	
265	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
266	
267	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
268	
269	## Workflow
270	
271	1. Read your directive completely.
272	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references. Track which local files you actually opened — you will need their paths for the `% SOURCE:` parenthetical.
273	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
274	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow. For each block that derives from an external source, identify the specific local `references/<file>.md` containing the verbatim statement (and proof, if applicable). If no local file covers the statement, list it as "retrieval needed" for the next step.
275	5. **For every "retrieval needed" item, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and **wait** for it to return. THEN open and read the newly-written `references/<slug>.md` before drafting the citing block. Do not draft citation blocks against pending or imagined sources.
276	6. Make the edits. For each citation block: copy `% SOURCE QUOTE:` and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` content character-by-character from the local reference file — original language, original notation, every word. Do not transcribe from a window of the file you "remember reading"; have the file open and copy.
277	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`). Spot-check that every `% SOURCE:` line has a non-empty `(read from references/<file>.md)` parenthetical and that the named file exists.
278	8. **Verify dependencies with `leandag`** (see the section below): confirm your chapter introduced no broken `\uses{}` and left none of your blocks unintentionally isolated. Fix the edges and re-check.
279	9. Write your report. List under "References consulted" every local file you opened in step 2 and step 5.
280	
281	## Verifying dependencies with leandag
282	
283	`leandag` parses the real Lean ↔ blueprint dependency DAG, so use it to check your `\uses{}` instead of trusting your memory of the graph. It is read-only on the blueprint (it never edits your chapter); run it after you make edits:
284	
285	```
286	leandag build --json                       # report: unknown_uses, unmatched_lean, isolated count
287	leandag query --isolated --chapter <c>     # isolated nodes in your chapter (no edges in or out)
288	leandag show isolated                       # all isolated nodes, project-wide
289	```
290	
291	`leandag` is on the same PATH as `archon`; it is the one tool you use to query the DAG.
292	
293	What to do with the output, scoped to **your chapter**:
294	
295	- **`unknown_uses` naming a label in your chapter** → you wrote a `\uses{}` pointing at a label that doesn't exist. Fix the label, or — if the target genuinely belongs in your chapter and the directive calls for it — add the missing declaration. If the missing label lives in another chapter, leave a correct `\uses{}` to it and note it under "Notes for Plan Agent" (you don't edit that chapter).
296	- **A block you wrote shows up as isolated** → it has no `\uses{}` out and nothing uses it. This is almost always a *missing edge*: add the `\uses{}` that ties it to the declarations its statement/proof actually depends on (and check whether a sibling result should `\uses{}` it). Only treat an isolated block as removable when your directive explicitly authorized removing it.
297	- **Removal** (directive-authorized only): delete the orphaned block cleanly and record it in your report. Never remove the goal or a block whose `\lean{}` still names a live Lean declaration.
298	
299	Keep `leandag` edits inside your assigned chapter — fixing a dependency that lives in another chapter is a "Notes for Plan Agent" item, not a cross-chapter edit.
300

303	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).

324	- **Removed** `<label>` — orphaned isolated block, removal authorized by directive.

Omit this section only when you wrote zero citation blocks (Archon-original chapter).>

2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).

26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).

81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.

84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:

91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.

152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155

158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.

33	/- Planner note:
34	   Blueprint `def:gr_affine_chart` (Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing together
35	   affine patches"): for `I ⊆ {1..r}` with `#I = d`, `X^I` is the `d×r` matrix
36	   whose `I`-minor is the `d×d` identity and whose other `d(r-d)` entries are
37	   independent indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` over ℤ. The affine chart is
38	   `U^I := Spec ℤ[X^I] = Spec (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)` —
39	   the spectrum of the polynomial ring on the `d(r-d)` free entries; non-canonically
40	   `≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. The prover should build `affineChart` as
41	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` of that `CommRingCat` (the MvPolynomial ring on the
42	   free-entry index type), via
43	   `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` or an equivalent index of cardinality
44	   `d(r-d)`. -/
45	
46	/-- The **affine chart** `U^I` of the Grassmannian `Gr(d, r)` indexed by a
47	`d`-element subset `I : Finset (Fin r)`.
48	
49	Concretely `U^I = Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{p : Fin d, q ∉ I}`, the spectrum of the
50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184	
185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]
198	
199	/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
200	applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`
201	(`def:gr_transition_pre`, `def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
202	theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
204	    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
205	      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
206	  ext p q
207	  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
208	  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
209	  · rw [dif_pos hq]
210	    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
211	    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
212	    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
213	      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
214	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
215	      rw [← hqk]; exact e
216	    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
217	    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
218	      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
219	      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
220	    by_cases hpk : p = k
221	    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
222	    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
223	  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]
224	
225	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to a unit (`lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`):
226	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) = det((X^I_J)⁻¹) = det(X^I_J)⁻¹ = 1/P^I_J`, a unit of `R^I_J`. This is the
227	hypothesis that lets `θ̃_{I,J}` extend along the away-localisation `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
228	Project-local. -/
229	theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
230	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
231	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
232	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
233	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
234	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
235	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
236	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
237	  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
238	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
239	  exact IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ hmul
240	
241	/-- The **transition map** `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`def:gr_transition`):
242	the away-localisation lift of the pre-hom `θ̃_{I,J}` along `P^J_I`, available because
243	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is a unit (`isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`). It is the comorphism of
244	the chart-overlap isomorphism `U^I_J → U^J_I`. Project-local. -/
245	noncomputable def transitionMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
246	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
247	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
248	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
249	    (g := (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
250	    (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
251	
252	/-- `θ_{I,I}` is the identity (`lem:gr_transition_self`): since `P^I_I = 1` the minor
253	`X^I_I = 1` is its own Cramer inverse, so the pre-hom is the structure map and its
254	away-localisation lift is the identity ring homomorphism. Project-local. -/
255	theorem transitionMap_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
256	    transitionMap d r I I hI hI = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
257	  have hmin1 : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
258	    simp only [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
259	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
260	  have hinv1 : universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
261	    simp only [universalMinorInv, hmin1]
262	    exact inv_one
263	  have himg : imageMatrix d r I I hI hI = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
264	    rw [imageMatrix, hinv1]
265	    exact Matrix.one_mul _
266	  have hpre : (transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI).toRingHom
267	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
268	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
269	    apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
270	    · intro n
271	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.C n) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.C n)
272	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_C,
273	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
274	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)), MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
275	    · intro e
276	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.X e) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.X e)
277	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, himg, Matrix.map_apply]
278	      congr 1
279	      rw [universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2]
280	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I I hI hI))
281	  simp only [transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.id_comp]
282	  exact hpre
283	
284	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — triple-overlap rings and the cocycle
285	
286	The cocycle condition `θ_{I,K} = θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K}` cannot be stated as a naive
287	composition of the landed `transitionMap`s: the codomain of `transitionMap d r J K`
288	(`R^J[1/P^J_K]`) differs from the domain of `transitionMap d r I J` (`R^J[1/P^J_I]`).
289	The identity therefore lives over the *triple-overlap* rings obtained by inverting BOTH
290	relevant minors in each chart ring:
291	`S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]`, `S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`, `S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
292	We build the localised transition maps `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`,
293	`Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I` over these rings and verify the cocycle.
294	
295	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
296	
297	/-- A ring homomorphism carries the nonsingular (Cramer) inverse to the nonsingular
298	inverse, provided the determinant is a unit. Project-local helper: Mathlib has no direct
299	`map`-`nonsingular inverse` compatibility lemma. -/
300	private lemma map_nonsing_inv {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
301	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
302	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
303	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
304	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
305	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
306	
307	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `x` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
308	(inverting the extra factor `y`). Project-local: the structure map of the triple overlap
309	relative to a double-localisation. -/
310	noncomputable def awayInclLeft {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
311	    Localization.Away x →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
312	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away x) x
313	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
314	    (by
315	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
316	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
317	      rw [map_mul] at h
318	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h)

326	    (by
327	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
328	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _

346	/-- The image of a general minor determinant `P^B_C` under the pre-localisation hom
347	`θ̃_{A,B}` is the determinant of the `C`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A`
348	(generalises the `C = A` computation underlying `lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`). Project-local. -/
349	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
350	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
351	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
352	      = ((imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
353	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det := by
354	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
355	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
356	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).map
357	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
358	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
359	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap]

361	/-- The "cross" minor `P^B_C` is sent by `θ̃_{A,B}`, then pushed into a double localisation
362	`D` (in which `P^A_C` is a unit), to a unit. Concretely
363	`θ̃_{A,B}(P^B_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹) · P^A_C`, a product of two units
364	once `P^A_C` is inverted. This is the cross-factor input to each triple-overlap transition
365	lift. Project-local. -/
366	private lemma isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross
367	    (d r : ℕ) (A B C : Finset (Fin r)) (hA : A.card = d) (hB : B.card = d) (hC : C.card = d)
368	    {D : Type*} [CommRing D] [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D]
369	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB) →+* D)
370	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
371	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))
372	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D)
373	    (hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D
374	        (minorDet d r A C hA hC))) :
375	    IsUnit (incl (transitionPreMap d r A B hA hB (minorDet d r B C hB hC))) := by
376	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r A B hA hB).submatrix id
377	        (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))
378	      = universalMinorInv d r A B hA hB *
379	        (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
380	          (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
381	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet, hsub, Matrix.det_mul, map_mul]
382	  refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
383	  · refine IsUnit.map incl ?_
384	    refine IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one (universalMinor d r A B hA hB).det ?_
385	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r A B hA hB).1, Matrix.det_one]
386	  · have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map
387	            (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))).submatrix id
388	            (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))).det
389	          = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC) := by
390	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]
391	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
392	    rw [hdet]
393	    have hcomp : incl (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
394	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC))
395	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D (minorDet d r A C hA hC) :=
396	      RingHom.congr_fun hincl _
397	    rw [hcomp]
398	    exact hunit

418	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, where
419	`S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`: the away-localisation lift of
420	`θ̃_{I,J}` (post-composed into `S_I`) along the doubly-inverted minor `P^J_I P^J_K`.
421	Project-local. -/
422	noncomputable def cocycleΘIJ (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
423	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
424	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) →+*
425	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
426	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
427	    (g := (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
428	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
429	    (by
430	      rw [map_mul]
431	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
432	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ).map
433	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
434	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I J K hI hJ hK
435	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
436	          (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
437	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_right _ _))

439	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`, where
440	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`. Project-local. -/
441	noncomputable def cocycleΘJK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
442	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
443	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
444	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) :=
445	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
446	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
447	            (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom)
448	    (by
449	      rw [map_mul]
450	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
451	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r J K I hJ hK hI
452	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))
453	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
454	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _)
455	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r J K hJ hK).map
456	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
457	
458	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I`, where
459	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`. Project-local. -/
460	noncomputable def cocycleΘIK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
461	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
462	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
463	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
464	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
465	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
466	            (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom)
467	    (by
468	      rw [map_mul]
469	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
470	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I K hI hK).map
471	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
472	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I K J hI hK hJ
473	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
474	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
475	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _))

477	/-- Mapping a base-changed matrix through a further ring hom collapses to a single base change
478	when the homs compose correctly. Project-local helper. -/
479	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
480	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
481	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
482	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]

484	/-- Mapping the image matrix `M = (X^I_X)⁻¹ X^I` through any ring hom `incl` lying over the
485	structure map `R^I → D` (i.e. `incl ∘ (R^I → R^I_X) = (R^I → D)`) yields `(Y_X)⁻¹ Y`, where
486	`Y := X^I` base-changed to `D`. The key reusable step in the cocycle computation. Project-local. -/
487	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
488	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
489	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
490	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
491	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
492	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
493	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
494	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
495	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
496	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
497	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
498	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
499	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
500	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
501	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
502	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
503	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
504	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
505	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
506	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
507	    ← map_nonsing_inv incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
508	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
509	  congr 1
510	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]

512	/-- Matrix cancellation `(B⁻¹ A)(A⁻¹ M) = B⁻¹ M` when `A` is invertible. Project-local helper
513	for the final step of the cocycle. -/
514	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
515	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
516	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
517	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
518	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]

520	/-- The central matrix identity behind the cocycle condition: over the triple-overlap ring
521	`S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied entrywise to the
522	image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. Both reduce to `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` with `Y = X^I` over

524	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)

526	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
527	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
528	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
529	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
530	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
531	  -- LHS = (Y_K)⁻¹ * Y, where `Y := X^I` over `S_I`.
532	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq d r I K hI hK
533	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
534	    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
535	  -- `(imageMatrix I J).map (awayInclLeft …) = (Y_J)⁻¹ Y`.

537	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))

540	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
541	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY

601	/-- **Cocycle condition** (`lem:gr_cocycle`): over the triple overlap, the transition maps satisfy
602	`Θ_{I,K} = Θ_{I,J} ∘ Θ_{J,K}` as ring homs `S_K →+* S_I`. Together with `θ_{I,I} = id`
603	(`transitionMap_self`) this is the gluing datum for the Grassmannian charts. -/
604	theorem cocycleCondition (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
605	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
606	    cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK
607	      = (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp (cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK) := by
608	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
609	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ))
610	  have hIK : (cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
611	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
612	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
613	      = (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
614	          (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom := by
615	    rw [cocycleΘIK]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
616	  have hJK : (cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
617	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ K}) ℤ)
618	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
619	      = (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
620	          (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom := by
621	    rw [cocycleΘJK]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
622	  rw [hIK, RingHom.comp_assoc, hJK]
623	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
624	  · intro n
625	    exact RingHom.congr_fun (RingHom.ext_int
626	      (((awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
627	        (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom).comp MvPolynomial.C)
628	      (((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
629	        ((awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
630	          (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom)).comp MvPolynomial.C)) n
631	  · intro e

1	\chapter{The Grassmannian over $\mathbb{Z}$: affine charts and gluing}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianCells}
3	
4	\section{Set-up: charts indexed by $d$-subsets}
5	\label{sec:gr_setup}
6	
7	Fix integers \(r \ge d \ge 1\). For a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M\) over a ring
8	and a subset \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\), write \(M_I\)
9	for the \emph{\(I\)-th minor} of \(M\): the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose
10	columns are those indexed by \(I\). We construct the Grassmannian scheme
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing one affine chart \(U^I\) for
12	each such \(I\). The construction is, conceptually, the quotient
13	\(\mathrm{GL}_{d,\mathbb{Z}} \backslash V\) of the scheme \(V\) of rank-\(d\)
14	matrices, but we never use the language of group-scheme actions: the charts and
15	their transition maps are elementary matrix algebra over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Taking
16	\(d = 1\) recovers \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,1) = \mathbb{P}^{r-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\).
17	
18	\begin{definition}
19	
20	\leanok
21	[Affine chart \(U^I\)]
22	  \label{def:gr_affine_chart}
23	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart}
24	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
25	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
26	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
27	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
28	  % "If $M$ is a $d\times r$-matrix, and
29	  %  $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with cardinality $\#(I)$ equal
30	  %  to $d$, the $I$ th minor $M_I$ of $M$ will
31	  %  mean the $d\times d$ minor of $M$ whose columns are indexed by
32	  %  $I$.
33	  %
34	  %  For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
35	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
36	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
37	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$.
38	  %  Let $\Z[X^I]$ denote the polynomial ring in the variables
39	  %  $x^I_{p,q}$, and let $U^I = \spec \Z[X^I]$, which is non-canonically
40	  %  isomorphic to the affine space $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$."
41	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
42	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). Let \(X^I\) denote the
43	  \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\)
44	  identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\) and whose remaining \(d(r - d)\) entries are
45	  independent indeterminates \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\),
46	  \(q \in \{1,\dots,r\} \setminus I\)) over \(\mathbb{Z}\). Write
47	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) for the polynomial ring in these \(d(r-d)\) variables and
48	  set
49	  \[
50	    U^I \;:=\; \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
51	  \]
52	  The choice of an ordering of the free entries gives a (non-canonical)
53	  isomorphism \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\); in particular
54	  \(U^I\) is a smooth affine \(\mathbb{Z}\)-scheme of relative dimension
55	  \(d(r-d)\). Intuitively a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-point of \(U^I\) is a \(d \times r\)
56	  matrix whose \(I\)-columns form the identity, i.e.\ a rank-\(d\) quotient of
57	  \(\mathbb{Z}^{r}\) trivialised on the coordinates \(I\).
58	\end{definition}
59	
60	\section{Transition maps and the cocycle condition}
61	\label{sec:gr_transition}
62	
63	\subsection*{Mathlib inputs for the transition map}
64	
65	The construction of \(\theta_{I,J}\) is a Cramer-inverse computation over the
66	localised chart ring, followed by one application of the universal property of a
67	localisation. We record the four Mathlib facts it rests on as dependency
68	anchors.
69	
70	\begin{lemma}
71	[Localised element is a unit]
72	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}

77	  away from \(x\) (i.e.\ \(S = R[1/x]\)). Then the image of \(x\) under the
78	  structure map \(R \to S\) is a unit of \(S\).
79	\end{lemma}

83	  \label{lem:mathlib_nonsing_inv_mul}

88	  nonsingular inverse \(A^{-1}\) satisfies \(A^{-1} A = 1\).

92	[Right Cramer inverse]
93	  \label{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}

95	  \mathlibok
96	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}

99	\end{lemma}

101	\begin{lemma}
102	[Universal property of an away-localisation]
103	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_lift}
104	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.lift}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
107	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S = R[1/x]\) a localisation
108	  of \(R\) away from \(x\). Given any ring homomorphism \(g : R \to P\) that
109	  sends \(x\) to a unit of \(P\), there is a unique ring homomorphism
110	  \(S \to P\) extending \(g\) along \(R \to S\).
111	\end{lemma}

113	\subsection*{The transition map by matrix algebra}
114	
115	We now build \(\theta_{I,J}\) in seven steps over the localised chart ring
116	\(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), splitting at the matrix-algebra seams. The
117	ambient chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) is the polynomial ring of
118	\cref{def:gr_affine_chart} in the \(d(r-d)\) free variables \(x^I_{p,q}\)
119	(\(p \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), \(q \notin I\)).

122	
123	\leanok
124	[The universal matrix \(X^I\)]
125	  \label{def:gr_universal_matrix}

129	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from

131	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
132	  % "For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
133	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
134	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
135	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$."
136	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). With the chart ring
137	  %   `R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, define
138	  %   `universalMatrix d r I : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R^I` whose entry at
139	  %   `(p, q)` is: the identity entry `if (orderIso I) p = q then 1 else 0`
140	  %   when `q ∈ I` (using the order isomorphism `Fin d ≃o I` from `#I = d`),
141	  %   and the indeterminate `MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩)` when `q ∉ I`.

144	  matrix} \(X^I\) is the \(d \times r\) matrix over the chart ring
145	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) whose \(I\)-minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\) identity

364	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
365	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
366	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
367	  % affine patches" (read from
368	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
369	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
370	  % "In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,

372	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target).
373	  %   `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J :`
374	  %   `IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J (minorDet d r J I))`.
375	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
376	  The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre})
377	  sends \(P^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}, the \(I\)-minor determinant of
378	  \(X^J\)) to a unit of \(R^I_J\). Indeed
379	  \[
380	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr)
381	      = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1} = 1/P^I_J,
382	  \]

389	  X^I\), so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries the \(I\)-minor \(X^J_I\) of \(X^J\) to
390	  the \(I\)-minor of \(M\), which is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) since
391	  the \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity. Hence \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) =
392	  \det((X^I_J)^{-1}) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\). As \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit
393	  (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), so is its inverse; the matrix-inverse
394	  determinant identity uses \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}.
395	\end{proof}

531	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$, and
532	% we leave it to the reader to verify that
533	% for any three subsets $I$, $J$ and $K$ of $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of
534	% cardinality $d$, the co-cycle condition
535	% $\theta_{I,K}= \theta_{I,J}\theta_{J,K}$
536	% is satisfied."  (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
537	% L838-L848.)
538	\begin{proof}
539	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_transition_self}
540	  \leanok
541	  We verify the scheme-morphism form
542	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) by a direct matrix
543	  computation, valid over the open locus of \(U^I\) on which all the minors
544	  involved are invertible (namely \(P^I_J\) and \(P^I_K\), which suffices since
545	  the maps are determined by their restriction to a dense open and all rings are
546	  domains; the computation is an identity of matrices with entries in
547	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J, 1/P^I_K]\)).
548	
549	  Start from the matrix \(X^I\) (the universal point of \(U^I\)). By
550	  \cref{def:gr_transition},
551	  \[
552	    M^J \;:=\; \theta_{I,J}(X^I) \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
553	  \]
554	  a \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(J\)-minor is the identity, hence a point of
555	  \(U^J\). Its \(K\)-minor is
556	  \[
557	    M^J_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_K,
558	    \qquad\text{so}\qquad
559	    (M^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J,
560	  \]
561	  using \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\) for the invertible \(d \times d\) matrices
562	  \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\). Applying \(\theta_{J,K}\) to \(M^J\) gives
563	  \[
564	    \theta_{J,K}(M^J) \;=\; (M^J_K)^{-1} M^J
565	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J \,\cdot\, (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I
566	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I,
567	  \]
568	  the middle product \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) cancelling by
569	  associativity of matrix multiplication. The right-hand side is precisely
570	  \(\theta_{I,K}(X^I) = (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I\). Thus
571	  \(\theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,K}\) agree on the
572	  universal matrix \(X^I\), hence agree as morphisms \(U^I \supseteq U^I_J \cap
573	  U^I_K \to U^K\). The special case \(K = I\) (resp.\ \(J = I\)) together with
574	  \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\) shows each \(\theta_{I,J}\) is an isomorphism onto its
575	  image with inverse \(\theta_{J,I}\), since
576	  \(\theta_{J,I} \circ \theta_{I,J} = \theta_{I,I} = \id\).
577	\end{proof}
